title: Acupuncture for Headache and Migraines
about: Some type of headache is thought to be caused by stress, tight muscles, lack of exercise, bad posture and eye strain. The causes of migraines aren’t fully known, but it may include hormonal changes, stress, sleep problems, stress, and blood pressure issues.
causes: Headaches and migraines are common neurological conditions characterized by recurrent episodes of head pain, often accompanied by other symptoms such as nausea, sensitivity to light and sound, and visual disturbances. While headaches can vary in intensity and duration, migraines are typically more severe and may last for hours to days, significantly impacting daily activities and quality of life. Various factors, including stress, hormonal changes, dietary triggers, and environmental factors, can contribute to the onset of headaches and migraines.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for headache and migraine relief. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as avoiding known triggers, practicing self-care techniques, or maintaining a headache diary to track symptoms and triggers. Additionally, maintain open communication with your acupuncturist to track your progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Arthritis
about: Arthritis affects the lining of cartilage within the joint, normally resulting and showing degeneration of cartilage. This makes movement more difficult, leading to pain and stiffness, which will progressively get worse over time if untreated, which are symptoms we commonly see.
causes: Arthritis is a common condition characterized by inflammation and stiffness in one or more joints, leading to pain, swelling, and reduced range of motion. There are several types of arthritis, including osteoarthritis (the most common form), rheumatoid arthritis, and psoriatic arthritis, each with its own causes and symptoms. Arthritis can affect people of all ages and lifestyles, impacting daily activities and quality of life.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and gentle movement to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for arthritis relief. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as applying heat or ice therapy, performing gentle stretching exercises, or using supportive devices such as splints or braces, to optimize pain relief and joint function. Additionally, maintain open communication with your acupuncturist to track your progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Plantar Fasciitis
about: It is normally caused by inflammation of a thick band of muscular tissue that runs across the bottom of the foot called the plantar fascia. The plantar fascia acts like a shock-absorbing bowstring. If tension and stress on that bowstring become too great, it can cause it to become inflamed and tight and can generate pain.
causes: Plantar fasciitis is a common foot condition characterized by inflammation and irritation of the plantar fascia, a thick band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot, connecting the heel bone to the toes. It is often caused by repetitive strain, overuse, or micro-tears in the plantar fascia, leading to heel pain, stiffness, and discomfort, especially with the first steps in the morning or after prolonged periods of rest. Plantar fasciitis can affect mobility and daily activities, making it challenging to stand, walk, or engage in physical activities.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and gentle movement to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for plantar fasciitis relief. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as using ice therapy, performing foot stretches, or wearing supportive footwear, to optimize pain relief and prevent recurrence of symptoms. Additionally, maintain open communication with your acupuncturist to track your progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Sciatica
about: Sciatica normally occurs because the sciatic nerve becomes trapped or has compression placed upon it, typically by the spine.
causes: Sciatica is a common condition characterized by pain that radiates along the sciatic nerve, which extends from the lower back down the back of each leg. It is typically caused by compression or irritation of the sciatic nerve, often due to a herniated disc, spinal stenosis, bone spurs, or muscle tension. Sciatica can cause sharp, shooting pain, tingling, numbness, or weakness in the lower back, buttocks, and legs, impacting mobility and daily activities.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and gentle movement to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for sciatica relief. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as heat or ice therapy, stretching exercises, or lifestyle modifications, to optimize pain relief and prevent recurrence of symptoms. Additionally, maintain open communication with your acupuncturist to track your progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Achilles Tendonitis
about:
Most of our patients typically feel a mild ache or pain in the back of the lower leg or above the heel after exercising. They can also experience tenderness or stiffness in the same area, normally in the morning, which usually improves with moderate movement.

Symptoms

Achilles tendonitis is typically caused by repetitive or intense strain on the Achilles tendon. This tendon is used when you walk, run, jump or push up on your toes. This means it is prone to wear and tear and seems to weaken with age, which can make it more susceptible to injury and can become inflamed.
causes: Achilles tendonitis is a common overuse injury characterized by inflammation and irritation of the Achilles tendon, which connects the calf muscles to the heel bone. It often occurs due to repetitive stress or strain on the tendon, such as excessive running, jumping, or sudden increases in physical activity. Achilles tendonitis can cause pain, stiffness, swelling, and tenderness along the back of the heel, affecting mobility and athletic performance.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, ice, compression, and elevation (RICE) to reduce inflammation and support your body's response to treatment. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as modifying activity levels or performing home exercises, to optimize recovery from Achilles tendonitis. Additionally, maintain open communication with your acupuncturist to track your progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Back Pain
about: Back pain normally caused by sitting for long periods of time or using the back too much when bending over, especially in compromised positions. Another possibility could be a strain on one of the connective tissues in the back, such as lifting a heavy object from the floor. The final possibility could be a skeletal problem in the back. This will feel like sharp pains during certain movements and positions such as twisting.
causes: Back pain is a common musculoskeletal complaint characterized by discomfort or discomfort in the upper, middle, or lower back. It can be caused by various factors, including muscle strain, poor posture, injury, herniated discs, spinal stenosis, arthritis, or underlying medical conditions. Back pain can range from mild to severe and may be acute (lasting less than 6 weeks) or chronic (lasting longer than 12 weeks), affecting daily activities and quality of life.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and gentle movement to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for back pain relief. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as heat or ice therapy, stretching exercises, or lifestyle modifications, to optimize pain relief and prevent recurrence of symptoms. Additionally, maintain open communication with your acupuncturist to track your progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

causes: Neck and shoulder pain are common musculoskeletal complaints that can be caused by various factors, including poor posture, muscle tension, injury, arthritis, stress, and underlying medical conditions. Symptoms may range from mild discomfort and stiffness to severe pain and limited range of motion, impacting daily activities and quality of life.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest and hydration to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for managing neck and shoulder pain. title: Acupuncture for Infertility
about: Discover how targeted acupuncture treatments at AcuPro Clinic can optimize your fertility. Our experienced acupuncturists work closely with fertility specialists to offer you a holistic approach to treating infertility, enhancing both natural conception and the success of assisted reproductive technologies like IVF and IUI. Infertility is the inability of a person to get pregnant or to impregnate someone or to carry a baby to full term.  People don’t produce the same quality of eggs as they used to, normally because the hormones needed to produce these eggs diminish, which is natural. Younger people may suffer from conditions that contribute to infertility. These conditions may include irregular periods, ovarian cysts, PCOS, PMS, fibrous tumor, fallopian tube blockages, and oocyte failure.
causes: Infertility is defined as the inability to conceive after one year of regular, unprotected intercourse (or six months for women over 35) despite no known reproductive issues. It can be caused by various factors, including hormonal imbalances, ovulation disorders, sperm abnormalities, fallopian tube blockages, uterine abnormalities, age-related decline in fertility, and lifestyle factors such as stress, obesity, or smoking. Infertility affects both men and women and can be emotionally challenging for couples seeking to start a family.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for fertility support. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as dietary adjustments or lifestyle modifications, to optimize your fertility potential. Additionally, maintain open communication with your fertility clinic and acupuncturist throughout the fertility treatment process to address any concerns and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Menstruation Cycle
about: Because of the changes going on, particularly with hormones, this can cause irregularity within the body and cause many undesirable conditions. From the point of view of Chinese patterns of disharmony, all of these symptoms can be traced to a stagnation of liver qi. Since the liver stores the blood and regulates the smooth flow of qi, it is almost always the organ involved in menstrual problems. When liver qi flows smoothly, the menstrual cycle is also smooth.
causes: The menstrual cycle is a natural process that occurs in reproductive-aged individuals and involves hormonal fluctuations and changes in the uterine lining to prepare for potential pregnancy. On average, the menstrual cycle lasts around 28 days, although variations are common. The menstrual cycle is divided into several phases, including menstruation (bleeding), the follicular phase, ovulation, and the luteal phase. Irregularities in the menstrual cycle, such as irregular periods, heavy or light bleeding, or absent periods, can indicate underlying health issues or hormonal imbalances.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for menstrual cycle regulation. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as dietary adjustments or lifestyle modifications, to optimize menstrual regularity and overall reproductive health. Additionally, maintain open communication with your acupuncturist to track your progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Menopause
about:
However, Chinese medicine actually considers menopausal symptoms as being pathological. If a woman is healthy and balanced, she should have a totally symptom-free cessation of her menstrual cycle.

A menopausal woman at onset of transition, a diagnostic pattern “Deficiency of Kidney Yin Resulting in Empty Heat” may be made in accordance with her clinical manifestations, such as hot flash, insomnia, night sweating or mood swing.

A pattern of “Deficiency of Kidney Yin and Kidney Yang” may be labeled later on when the postmenopausal stage is attained, become elder.
causes: Menopause is a natural biological process that occurs in women typically in their late 40s to early 50s, marked by the cessation of menstruation and a decline in reproductive hormones, particularly estrogen and progesterone. The primary cause of menopause is the aging of the ovaries, leading to a decrease in ovarian follicles and hormonal changes.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support the body's healing process and enhance the effectiveness of your treatment. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as dietary adjustments or lifestyle changes, to optimize symptom management and promote overall well-being during menopause. Additionally, schedule regular follow-up appointments to monitor progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Wrinkles
about: As the needles puncture the skin, they create wounds within its threshold, which are called positive microtraumas. When your body senses these wounds, it goes into repair mode. This is the same idea microneedling uses to get bright, anti-aging results — except acupuncture is a bit less intense, averaging about 50 punctures. This helps improve elasticity, minimizing fine lines and wrinkles.
causes: Wrinkles are a natural part of the aging process, characterized by the formation of fine lines, creases, and folds in the skin. The primary causes of wrinkles include decreased production of collagen and elastin fibers in the skin, exposure to ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the sun, repetitive facial expressions, smoking, poor nutrition, and environmental factors such as pollution and stress.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize gentle skincare practices, such as using hydrating serums and moisturizers, to support the skin's healing process and enhance the effectiveness of your treatment. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as dietary adjustments or lifestyle changes, to optimize skin health and maintain a youthful complexion. Additionally, schedule regular follow-up appointments to monitor progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Eczema: A Holistic Approach to Skin Health
about: Eczema is a chronic condition causing itchy, red, dry, and cracked skin. While it often begins in childhood, it can persist into adulthood. At AcuPro Clinic, we combine acupuncture and herbal medicine to provide effective relief for eczema symptoms. We normally see patients that have suffered from the condition since childhood with a variety of symptoms. Eczema is a condition we see more than you think, and we treat it with a high success rate. Eczema is typically a genetic condition that can be triggered by stress. Some people’s eczema may flare up because of poor digestion and from certain foods that they may be allergic too. The other group of people’s eczema may flare up from poor sleep quality which can either be not enough sleep or irregular sleep. There are typical symptoms we see and treat for eczema & has normally been there since childhood.
causes:
Eczema results from a mix of genetic and environmental factors.

Common triggers include allergens, stress, poor digestion, and inadequate sleep. Eczema, also known as atopic dermatitis, is a chronic inflammatory skin condition characterised by red, itchy, and inflamed patches of skin.

The exact cause of eczema is not fully understood, but it is believed to result from a combination of genetic, environmental, and immune system factors. Triggers for eczema flare-ups may include allergens, irritants, dry skin, stress, and certain foods.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, continue to follow your skincare routine and implement any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist to support eczema management and promote skin healing. Additionally, schedule regular follow-up appointments to monitor progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for Acne
about:
Cystic acne is thought to be down to the stagnation of blood. Whitehead/pustules acne normally tend to reoccur every 3-4 weeks and are more prevalent in females due to hormonal changes as it is a result of their monthly cycle. Both of these conditions normally occur from inflamed or infected glands within the skin. This is down to bacteria that have formed from excess sweat or oil.

Symptoms

Cystic acne is where the lumps/spots are painful when pressing and are typically widespread in a certain area with a reddish colour, Whitehead/pustules acne normally have smaller red or white spots which are puss-filled and can be popped
causes: Acne is a common skin condition characterized by the presence of pimples, blackheads, whiteheads, and inflamed lesions on the skin. The primary causes of acne include excess oil production, clogged pores, bacteria, inflammation, hormonal fluctuations, and genetic predisposition. Hormonal acne, specifically, is often triggered by fluctuations in hormone levels, while cystic acne is a severe form of acne characterized by deep, painful cysts beneath the skin's surface.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, continue to follow your skincare routine and implement any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist to support acne management and promote skin healing. Additionally, schedule regular follow-up appointments to monitor progress and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for IVF
about: Preparing for IVF with Acupuncture and Herbal Medicine Optimise Your Fertility Pre-IVF: If you are considering starting IVF, incorporating herbal medicine and Moxibustion (Moxi-Acupuncture) can be beneficial. Taking 2-3 months to adjust your internal environment can significantly improve egg quality and enhance overall conception chances. This preparatory phase is crucial for optimising your body's reproductive health. Acupuncture During Your IVF Cycle: Once your IVF treatment begins, especially after starting hormone injections, it's advisable to adjust your treatment approach. Herbal medicine may not be recommended in conjunction with hormone treatments due to potential interactions. Instead, Moxi-Acupuncture becomes the optimal choice for supporting follicle nourishment and endometrial lining growth. Regular sessions, typically 1-2 times per week, can enhance these effects. Tailored Acupuncture Points for Each IVF Phase: Regardless of your stage in the IVF process, the selection of acupuncture points and treatment strategies vary significantly between the follicular phase and the luteal phase of your cycle. It's imperative to communicate the specifics of your menstrual cycle to your practitioner. This enables a more tailored and effective treatment plan, ensuring that you receive the most appropriate acupuncture care tailored to your IVF timeline. Why Tailored Acupuncture Matters: Tailored acupuncture treatments ensure that each phase of your IVF process is supported by specific techniques that enhance reproductive health and improve the chances of a successful pregnancy. This personalised approach is critical in managing the unique hormonal and physiological changes that occur throughout IVF treatment.
causes: In Vitro Fertilization (IVF) is a fertility treatment where eggs are fertilized by sperm outside the body in a laboratory. It's a common assisted reproductive technology used to help individuals and couples conceive when natural conception is difficult or not possible. IVF involves several steps, including ovarian stimulation, egg retrieval, fertilization, embryo culture, and embryo transfer.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for IVF support. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as dietary adjustments or lifestyle modifications, to optimize your chances of success during the IVF cycle. Additionally, maintain open communication with your fertility clinic and acupuncturist throughout the process to address any concerns and adjust your treatment plan as needed.

title: Acupuncture for PMS
about: Painful menstruation is also called dysmenorrhea. There are two types of dysmenorrhea: primary and secondary. Primary dysmenorrhea occurs in people who experience pain before and during menstruation. If you’ve had normal periods that become painful later in life, it may be secondary dysmenorrhea. A condition affecting the uterus or other pelvic organs, such as endometriosis or uterine fibroids, can cause this. The herbal medicine and acupuncture mean to regulate and balance the hormone to reduce the PMS level.
causes: Premenstrual Syndrome (PMS) refers to a combination of physical, emotional, and psychological symptoms that occur in the days or weeks before menstruation. Common symptoms of PMS include mood swings, irritability, bloating, breast tenderness, fatigue, headaches, and food cravings. While the exact cause of PMS is not fully understood, hormonal fluctuations, neurotransmitter imbalances, and lifestyle factors may contribute to its development.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for managing PMS symptoms. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as dietary adjustments or lifestyle modifications, to optimize symptom relief and overall menstrual health. title: Acupuncture for Labour Assist
causes: Overdue labour, also known as post-term pregnancy, refers to a pregnancy that extends beyond 42 weeks gestation. While it's common for pregnancies to last between 37 to 42 weeks, some pregnancies may continue beyond the expected due date. Overdue labour can pose risks to both the mother and the baby, including increased likelihood of complications such as macrosomia (large birth weight), meconium aspiration, and placental insufficiency.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest and hydration to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for promoting labour onset. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as gentle movement and positioning techniques, to encourage the progression of labour. Acupuncture is a complementary and alternative medicine that is commonly used as adjuvant therapy in many clinical conditions to reduce adverse drug reaction and improve treatment. Studies have confirmed the effectiveness of acupuncture in relieving the symptoms of IBS
causes: Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is a gastrointestinal disorder characterized by abdominal pain, bloating, and changes in bowel habits. The exact cause of IBS is not fully understood, but factors such as abnormal gastrointestinal motility, hypersensitivity to certain foods, stress, and alterations in gut microbiota may contribute to its development.

title: Acupuncture for Sperm Quality
about: Low sperm count, also called oligospermia, is a major cause of male infertility. Sperm count is considered low if it dips below 15 million sperm per milliliter (mL) of semen, although the average is around 75 million sperm per mL.
causes: Understanding the factors that contribute to poor sperm quality is essential for addressing male infertility issues. Common causes include hormonal imbalances, lifestyle factors such as smoking or excessive alcohol consumption, environmental toxins, and certain medical conditions like varicocele or infections.

title: Acupuncture for Insomnia
about:
Sleep is part of the natural rhythm of yin and yang qi in the body. Yin and yang qi play opposite roles – in the day, yang qi dominates as it fuels the body’s activities and keeps us warm and alert.

However, as day turns into night, the cooling, quiet and nourishing yin qi dominates. It helps slow down the body’s activity so that it is relaxed and calm for a good night’s sleep. When this natural rhythm is disrupted, there is an imbalance of yin and yang qi, which results in insomnia.
causes: Insomnia can be caused by various factors, including stress, anxiety, depression, poor sleep habits, underlying medical conditions, medications, and lifestyle factors such as caffeine or alcohol consumption. Understanding the root causes of insomnia is essential for effective treatment and management.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support the body's healing process and enhance the effectiveness of your treatment. title: Mesotherapy
about:
Economic Solution

Dermaheal HSR rejuvenates aged and dehydrated skin. It also contains powerful antioxidants and stimulates collagen remodelling, as well as reduces hyperpigmentation. Benefits Dermaheal HSR: Reduces fine lines; Strengthens skin elasticity; Hydrates dry skin and helps to maintain its optimum moisture balance. Dermaheal SB is designed to reduce and prevent hyper-pigmentation and fight age spots. It also works to brighten and effectively prevent further skin pigmentation, leaving skin smooth, soft and radiant. Benefits of Dermaheal SB: Treatment areas: sun damage, sun spots, freckles and uneven skin tone Dermaheal SR is a powerful anti-ageing facial treatment which rejuvenates ageing skins and reduces the appearance of wrinkles and fine lines. It contains hyaluronic acid to moisturise skin and shrink pores. Benefits of Dermaheal SR: Revitalises dry and dull skin; Promotes the production of collagen and elastin; Makes skin firmer, softer, smoother, brighter and younger looking

Luxury Solution

NCTF® 135HA is a unique, Positive Pro-ageing, polyrevitalising solution that provides the dermis with the essential ingredients it needs to thrive. It creates the optimal environment to reboost fibroblasts activity, which are responsible for the production of collagen, elastin and hyaluronic acid. NCTF® 135HA stimulates the skin without changing the facial features or expressions. Containing free HA, the New Cellular Treatment Factor (NCTF®) smoothes out fine lines, restores radiance, deeply nourishes the skin and increases density of the dermis. Cytocare 532 is a resorbable implant composed of hyaluronic acid and a rejuvenating complex. Cytocare 532 is designed to be injected into the mid-deep layer of the skin dermis to fight the skin's ageing process and reduce fine lines and wrinkles. Cytocare 532 also restores the hydration, tone and elasticity of the skin and improves radiance. Neauvia® Organic Hydro Deluxe Mesotherapy is one of the most popular aesthetic treatments that significantly improve quality of the skin. The aim of it is to deliver active substances directly to the skin and to give an immediate hydration. The success of the treatment depends to a great extend from the quality of the product injected and the repeated sessions.   INNO-TDS Restructurer® significantly reduces the formation of free radicals, protecting the skin from cell oxidation and oxidative stress. Therefore, it effectively slows down the skin aging process, attenuates fine lines and wrinkles, and evens the skin tone. Active ingredients: Ascorbic Acid, Glutathione, Superoxide dismutase, Lipoic Acid, Hyaluronic Acid Key benefits: Restructuring, Anti-aging, Free radical scavenger, Balances uneven pigmentation, Prevents cell oxidation.

title: Thermage FLX®
about:
Thermage®

Thermage® is a monopolar, capacitively coupled

radiofrequency system, that utilizes a reverse thermal

gradient, to non-invasively help smooth, tighten and

contour skin.

It can also temporarily reduce the appearance of cellulite

on the body.

body:
Why Thermage FLX® at AcuPro by Montecosmet® ?

At AcuPro, we adopt our very own unique transdermal revitalisation protocol to address signs of aging such as fine lines, loss of elasticity and dullness of the skin by targeting at all layers of the skin. Thermage FLX® uses monopolar radiofrequency technology to heat the deeper layers of the skin, causes collagen fibres to contract, resulting in immediate skin tightening and collagen remodelling. The skin after a single treatment is smoother and tighter, with results that continue to improve over time. In order to achieve the optimum result, our Counter Aging Expert uses ultrasonic hydrodermabrasion to enhance the skin hydration, which can boost the collagen production after the Thermage FLX® treatment. LED light therapy combined with our Global Age Defence Ultra Lifting Serum will further reduce the fine lines and soothe the skin after the treatment, minimising the downtime to almost zero. 

How Thermage FLX® works ?

Collagen is a protein that is found throughout the body, accounting for approximately 30% of total protein content. It plays a critical role in maintaining the structure and elasticity of the skin. In the skin, collagen fibres form a network that helps to maintain the skin's structure and prevent thinning, sagging and wrinkling. On average, it takes about three to four weeks for the body to produce new collagen. However, collagen synthesis in aged human skin may take up to twice as long as in young skin. As we age, the production and quality of collagen begins to decline, which leads to a loss of elasticity and support in the skin. It can also be exacerbated by environmental factors such as sun exposure and smoking, which can damage existing collagen fibres and decrease the production of new collagen. However, there are a number of ways that can be used to help maintain collagen production and prevent these changes

Other than leading a healthy lifestyle and the use of skincare products that stimulate collagen synthesis, high-grade radiofrequency therapy stimulates the growth of collagen, giving better contour to the areas treated and delay aging process. Thermage FLX® Skin Tightening is the world-leading non-invasive skin tightening treatment designed to reverse aging process by using radiofrequency energy to heat the dermal layers, triggering a natural collagen response that stimulates the production of new collagen fibres. The radiofrequency energy used in Thermage FLX® penetrates the skin's surface to heat the deep layers of the skin, also known as the dermis. The heat causes the collagen fibres in the dermis to contract, creating an immediate and lasting tightening effects.

title: Mesotherapy / Microneedling
body:
INTRODUCTION

Facial Mesotherapy is an innovative technique to rejuvenate the skin and improve its health and appearance. The treatment delivers a tailored cocktail of nutrients such as hyaluronic acid, vitamins, minerals and FDA-approved pharmaceuticals into the lower layer of your skin.

title: Acupuncture for Infertility GAD
about: Discover how targeted acupuncture treatments at AcuPro Clinic can optimize your fertility. Our experienced acupuncturists work closely with fertility specialists to offer you a holistic approach to treating infertility, enhancing both natural conception and the success of assisted reproductive technologies like IVF and IUI. Infertility is the inability of a person to get pregnant or to impregnate someone or to carry a baby to full term.  People don’t produce the same quality of eggs as they used to, normally because the hormones needed to produce these eggs diminish, which is natural. Younger people may suffer from conditions that contribute to infertility. These conditions may include irregular periods, ovarian cysts, PCOS, PMS, fibrous tumor, fallopian tube blockages, and oocyte failure.
causes: Infertility is defined as the inability to conceive after one year of regular, unprotected intercourse (or six months for women over 35) despite no known reproductive issues. It can be caused by various factors, including hormonal imbalances, ovulation disorders, sperm abnormalities, fallopian tube blockages, uterine abnormalities, age-related decline in fertility, and lifestyle factors such as stress, obesity, or smoking. Infertility affects both men and women and can be emotionally challenging for couples seeking to start a family.
aftercare: After each acupuncture session, prioritize rest, hydration, and relaxation to support your body's response to treatment and enhance the effectiveness of acupuncture for fertility support. Follow any recommendations provided by your acupuncturist, such as dietary adjustments or lifestyle modifications, to optimize your fertility potential. Additionally, maintain open communication with your fertility clinic and acupuncturist throughout the fertility treatment process to address any concerns and adjust your treatment plan as needed.
